text:
Fold `whoami`'s useful output into `who`'s agent-context (fabric) branch:

1. **In `rpc_who` (`src/daemon/server/who.rs:66-94`)**, where the caller's session already resolves for `(self_slug, self_pubkey)`: also compute `let instance = state.session_instance(&rec)` and attach a small `self` identity object to the `out` JSON (`label = instance.display_slug()`, `pubkey = instance.pubkey`, `is_member`, `pending`, `host`, `rel_cwd`) — reusing the exact field logic from `rpc_whoami` (who.rs:130-161). Use `self_pubkey = instance.pubkey` rather than `rec.agent_pubkey` so the `(you)` member match (summary.rs:148) keys on the ordinal-selected pubkey (issue #98 correctness).
2. **In `render_fabric_view` / `render_snapshot_body` (`src/cli/who/awareness.rs:43-105`)**, prepend a one-line self header ("You are **{label}** on **{channel}** ({host})") before `Project:`/`Channel:`, driven by the new `self` block. This makes `tenex-edge who`, when run inside an agent session, answer "who am I, which channel, my instance label/pubkey" — exactly `whoami`'s job.
3. The `whoami` renderer `render_whoami` (render.rs:67-129) and its `rendering.rs` test become the spec for what the new self-header must contain (agent/label, project/channel, host, pubkey, status, member, pending). [...] - Deleted `render_whoami`; replaced it with `render_self_header(v) -> Option<String>` driven by `out["self"]` — a concise leading line `You are **{label}** on **{channel}** ({host}).` plus `pubkey … · status … · member yes/no · N pending`. Never shows a codename or raw session id.
- Wired the header into both `who` (printed before the fabric block) and `who_live` (prepended to the live fabric screen). [...] 2. **In `render_fabric_view` / `render_snapshot_body` (`src/cli/who/awareness.rs:43-105`)**, prepend a one-line self header ("You are **{label}** on **{channel}** ({host})") before `Project:`/`Channel:`, driven by the new `self` block. This makes `tenex-edge who`, when run inside an agent session, answer "who am I, which channel, my instance label/pubkey" — exactly `whoami`'s job.
3. The `whoami` renderer `render_whoami` (render.rs:67-129) and its `rendering.rs` test become the spec for what the new self-header must contain (agent/label, project/channel, host, pubkey, status, member, pending). [...] - Deleted `render_whoami`; replaced it with `render_self_header(v) -> Option<String>` driven by `out["self"]` — a concise leading line `You are **{label}** on **{channel}** ({host}).` plus `pubkey … · status … · member yes/no · N pending`. Never shows a codename or raw session id. [...] - Deleted `display_row_agent_name`, `agent_name_counts_for_scope`, and `agent_count_key` (the entire codename-disambiguation apparatus).
- `render_who_row` and `render_who_markdown_row` now render `row.slug` directly (concurrent same-agent instances carry distinct ordinal slugs, e.g. `haiku`/`haiku1`); their `name_counts`/`split_counts_by_project` params and the corresponding setup in `render_who_once`/`render_who_plain` were removed. Removed the `session_codename` reference.
 [...] VERIFIED for B: `WhoRow.slug` is populated from `s.agent_slug` (local rows) and `st.slug`/`peer_slug` (peer rows) in `snapshot.rs` — the status slug, which post-#98 equals the ordinal display label, so rendering `row.slug` directly is correct.
 [...] The emitted payload is `{"session_id": canonical, "codename": codename}`.

**Concrete replacement:** **DELETE** the `codename` field (293) and its computation (290), emitting `{"session_id": canonical}` only. The caller (opencode plugin) parses `session_id` for adoption; the `codename` was display-only per the comment.

**Caller breakage:** The consumer is the opencode TS plugin and the integration test `tests/daemon_integration/process.rs:84–89`, which already reads `v["session_id"]` and ignores `codename`. Removing the field does **not** break parsing. The comment at 288–289 should be updated. [...] **Returned `codename` JSON (no in-repo consumer found** — grep for `["codename"]`/`.codename` reads is empty): per the directive, **DELETE all of these:**
- `src/cli/hooks.rs:290,293` — echo-session-id response **[WIRE]**
- `src/daemon/server/session_start.rs:314` (re-assert return) & `:472` (start return) **[WIRE]**
- `src/daemon/server/who.rs:151` — `whoami` response **[WIRE]** (the same RPC already returns `label` from `instance.display_slug()` at `:149`, which is the first-class replacement)
 [...] - **session_start / hooks responses + logs** (me) ✅ — `codename` field & log dropped
- **slog filename** (me) ✅ — raw `session_id` [...] - `src/idref.rs:46-55` `session_label` — `"codename (agent@host)"`, consumed by `format_envelope` (`src/cli/messaging.rs:235`). **[USER-FACING]** Replace with `agent_label`/instance `display_slug` only (drop the codename prefix).
 [...] - `session_label` no longer renders `codename (agent@host)` — it returns the agent-instance label `agent@host` (degrading to the raw `session_id` only when slug is empty). This is what `format_envelope` consumes, so the envelope "From" line is now codename-free automatically.
- Deleted `token_is_codename`; dropped the `looks_like_codename`/`session_codename` imports (now only `slugify_host`).
